pasteImageInternal.path.description: The destination to save image file. Supports variables such as ${currentFileDir}, ${currentFileDirName}, ${currentFileParentDir}, ${currentFileParentDirName}, ${projectRoot}, ${projectRootName}, ${currentFileName}, ${currentFileNameWithoutExt}, and ${currentFileExt}.

pasteImageInternal.basePath.description: The base path of image url. Supports the same current-file variables as pasteImageInternal.path. If you set basePath to empty string, it will insert an absolute path to file.

pasteImageInternal.forceUnixStyleSeparat.description: Force set the file separator styel to unix style. If set false, separator styel will follow the system style.

pasteImageInternal.prefix.description: The string prepend to the resolved image path before paste.

pasteImageInternal.suffix.description: The string append to the resolved image path before paste.

pasteImageInternal.defaultName.description: The default image file name. The value of this config will be passed to the moment format function. Supports current-file variables before formatting.

pasteImageInternal.namePrefix.description: The string prepended to the image file name. Supports current-file variables such as ${currentFileDirName}.

pasteImageInternal.nameSuffix.description: The string appended to the image file name. Supports current-file variables.

pasteImageInternal.encodePath.description: The string append to the image file name.How to encode image path before insert to editor. Support options: none, urlEncode, urlEncodeSpace

pasteImageInternal.insertPattern.description: The pattern of string that would be pasted to text. Supports image variables and current-file variables.

pasteImageInternal.showFilePathConfirmIn.description: Set to true if you want to be able to change the file path or name prior to saving the file to disk

pasteImageInternal.filePathConfirmInputB.description: Set the mode of file path confirm inputbox

scripts.test: npm run vscode:prepublish && node ./node_modules/mocha/bin/mocha --ui tdd out/test/unit/*.test.js
